event_description: An airborne camera tracks a passenger bus driving along a multi-lane highway. The EO view provides a consistent visual profile. The primary challenge arises in the IR view, where the thermal contrast of the bus fluctuates significantly; it appears distinct against cooler agricultural fields but experiences reduced contrast when passing over warmer, sun-heated pavement and infrastructure (like overpasses and intersections).

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with bus continuity across changing backgrounds; warm pavement and infrastructure nearby
options.B: the object with warm pavement and infrastructure; bus continuity across changing backgrounds nearby
options.C: the object with lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; roadside shadows or field texture nearby
options.D: the object with roadside shadows or field texture; road-following motion through scale changes nearby

qa.evidence_note: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which motion cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: warm pavement and infrastructure; view scale or angle changes
options.B: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; view scale or angle changes
options.C: roadside shadows or field texture; view scale or angle changes
options.D: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings
options.B: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; warm pavement and infrastructure
options.C: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; bus continuity across changing backgrounds
options.D: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; roadside shadows or field texture

qa.evidence_note: thermal contrast changes as the bus crosses cooler fields and warmer pavement

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: roadside shadows or field texture; near the object
options.B: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; near the object
options.C: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; near the object
options.D: warm pavement and infrastructure; near the object

qa.evidence_note: using thermal contrast change as an identity switch

qa.question: Which EO/IR cue pair is visible?

options.A: EO: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; IR: roadside shadows or field texture
options.B: EO: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.C: EO: warm pavement and infrastructure; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.D: EO: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background

qa.evidence_note: EO: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway; IR: thermal contrast changes as the bus crosses cooler fields and warmer pavement

qa.question: Which cue pairing continues through camera/view change?

options.A: the object: warm pavement and infrastructure; screen scale or angle changes
options.B: the object: roadside shadows or field texture; screen scale or angle changes
options.C: the object: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; screen scale or angle changes
options.D: the object: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; screen scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway

qa.question: Which EO cue is aligned with the IR contrast cue?

options.A: EO cue: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; IR cue: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.B: EO cue: roadside shadows or field texture; IR cue: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.C: EO cue: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; IR cue: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.D: EO cue: warm pavement and infrastructure; IR cue: vehicle heat against pavement or field background

qa.evidence_note: EO: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway; IR: thermal contrast changes as the bus crosses cooler fields and warmer pavement

qa.question: Which competing cue appears in the cross-modal chain?

options.A: EO/visible cue: warm pavement and infrastructure; IR/contrast context: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.B: EO/visible cue: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; IR/contrast context: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.C: EO/visible cue: roadside shadows or field texture; IR/contrast context: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.D: EO/visible cue: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; IR/contrast context: vehicle heat against pavement or field background

qa.evidence_note: using thermal contrast change as an identity switch

qa.question: When the view changes, which cue is checked against the nearby alternative?

options.A: warm pavement and infrastructure; bus continuity across changing backgrounds
options.B: roadside shadows or field texture; bus continuity across changing backgrounds
options.C: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; warm pavement and infrastructure
options.D: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; warm pavement and infrastructure

qa.evidence_note: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway

qa.question: Which IR cue pairing appears in this interval?

options.A: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; warm pavement and infrastructure
options.B: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings
options.C: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; bus continuity across changing backgrounds
options.D: vehicle heat against pavement or field background; roadside shadows or field texture

qa.evidence_note: thermal contrast changes as the bus crosses cooler fields and warmer pavement

qa.question: Which combined EO/IR sequence account is visible?

options.A: EO: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.B: EO: roadside shadows or field texture; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.C: EO: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background
options.D: EO: warm pavement and infrastructure; IR: vehicle heat against pavement or field background

qa.evidence_note: EO: the bus profile stays consistent while it travels along the highway; IR: thermal contrast changes as the bus crosses cooler fields and warmer pavement

qa.question: For the full clip, which cue chain is visible?

options.A: the object: warm pavement and infrastructure; nearby cue: bus continuity across changing backgrounds
options.B: the object: lane edges, shoulders, fields, or road markings; nearby cue: bus continuity across changing backgrounds
options.C: the object: bus continuity across changing backgrounds; nearby cue: warm pavement and infrastructure
options.D: the object: roadside shadows or field texture; nearby cue: warm pavement and infrastructure

qa.evidence_note: using thermal contrast change as an identity switch

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
